steps.title: Reconcile the two studies
steps.content: Rao and Chen found no enduring (long-lasting) increases in investment after liberalization. Grant found a significant increase specifically in the three years following liberalization. Together, these findings suggest investment rose briefly after liberalization but did not persist — a temporary boost that fades.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• "liberalization may provide a boost to investment that fades over time.": Directly reconciles both studies: a short-term increase (Grant) with no lasting effect (Rao and Chen). ✓
  • "economists’ expectations about the effect of liberalization on investment were largely correct.": The passage never mentions what economists expected — this is unsupported. ✗
  • "companies typically do not benefit from liberalization until at least three years after liberalization occurs.": Misreads Grant's finding — the significant increase happened in the three years following liberalization, not after a delay. ✗
  • "investment growth is likely to be more consistent in countries that liberalize than in countries that do not.": Neither study compares liberalized to non-liberalized countries. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
